sanskrit: अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् । पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥
transliteration: aparyāptaṃ tad asmākaṃ balaṃ bhīṣmābhirakṣitam | paryāptaṃ tv idam eteṣāṃ balaṃ bhīmābhirakṣitam ||
literal_meaning: Our strength is immeasurable, protected by Bhishma; their strength is limited, protected by Bhima.
contextual_meaning: Duryodhana’s boast reveals the ego’s need to inflate self-worth by diminishing others, masking deep insecurity behind grandiosity. Psychologically, it shows how comparison and projection create an illusion of invulnerability that actually signals inner dread of defeat.
psychological_state: A leader who loudly proclaims their team’s superiority while secretly fearing they will be exposed as inadequate.
trigger_scenario: A manager about to present against a rival department, telling colleagues "We’ve got the best data and the senior VP on our side; their proposal is weak," yet lying awake rehearsing rebuttals to stave off panic.
anchor_text: True strength needs no comparison; ego’s armor is fear in disguise.
